name: Prompt Engineering Mastery
description: Master the craft of designing, testing, and iterating prompts for production LLM applications. From basic principles to advanced patterns — few-shot, chain-of-thought, structured output, RAG prompts, and agentic tool-use.
targetAudience: Engineers, product managers, and technical leads who build or oversee AI-powered features

steps.title: Prompt Design Fundamentals
steps.description: Understand the core anatomy of a prompt: instructions, context, examples, output format. Learn what the model sees vs what you write, and the impact of each element.

substeps.title: Study: Instruction tuning and how models follow directions
Experiment: Zero-shot vs one-shot vs few-shot prompting
Measure: Track quality changes as you modify each element

steps.title: Chain-of-Thought and Reasoning Patterns
steps.description: Use chain-of-thought (CoT), self-consistency, and step-back prompting to significantly improve reasoning quality for complex tasks.

substeps.title: Learn: Why CoT works and when to use it
Build: Standard vs CoT prompts for a logic problem
Build: Self-consistency sampling for higher reliability
Verify: Accuracy improvement on a test set

steps.title: Structured Output & Schema-Driven Prompting
steps.description: Reliably extract structured data from LLMs. Use JSON mode, function calling, and schema-based constraints to get predictable machine-readable output.

substeps.title: Study: JSON mode, function calling, and structured outputs API
Build: Extract structured data from unstructured text
Build: Retry logic for malformed outputs
Verify: Parse success rate over 50 samples

steps.title: RAG Prompt Patterns
steps.description: Design prompts for Retrieval-Augmented Generation: format retrieved context, handle conflicting sources, instruct the model to cite evidence, and avoid hallucination.

substeps.title: Study: RAG architecture and where prompting fits
Build: Context injection templates with source citation
Build: Prompt that handles conflicting retrieved documents
Verify: Hallucination rate with/without citation instructions

steps.title: Prompt Safety & Red-Teaming
steps.description: Harden prompts against adversarial inputs: prompt injection, jailbreaks, data exfiltration. Learn systematic red-teaming techniques and how to build robust system prompts.

substeps.title: Study: Common prompt injection and jailbreak patterns
Red-team: 10 adversarial attacks on your own prompts
Build: Defense strategies (input validation, output checks)
Verify: Before/after security posture

steps.title: Prompt Testing, Versioning & Iteration
steps.description: Treat prompts as production artifacts. Set up a prompt registry, automated eval pipeline, A/B testing, and regression testing so you can iterate without regressions.

substeps.title: Design: Prompt versioning strategy (file, DB, or registry)
Build: Automated eval harness for your prompt suite
Build: A/B test two prompt versions on 50 samples
Verify: Catch a regression using your eval pipeline
